summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates developer identity and git/GitHub credentials to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned identity files the package did not create — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — to extract the developer's git email and GitHub login. It additionally collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), and environment variables (GIT_AUTHOR_EMAIL, EMAIL, CI-related vars), then POSTs the combined payload as JSON to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ via https.request at line 161. The README claims telemetry is limited to 'platform, Node version, architecture' with 'no credentials transmitted' — this is a cover story contradicted by the code. The package name 'openai-agents-helpers' and README links to openai.github.io imply official OpenAI affiliation, but the publisher is an unrelated party at openai-agents-js.guide, leveraging the OpenAI Agents SDK brand to lure installs. The combination of brand impersonation, installer-secret-file reads, and exfiltration to an unrelated Cloud Run endpoint is install-time identity harvesting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
